project.name: johnvideo
project.description: A simple native macOS video editor written in C, using FFmpeg for decode/encode/mux, with an Objective-C++ AppKit UI shell.

decisions.why: User chose native macOS; AppKit can't be driven from pure C, so UI is .mm with a pure-C engine.
Engine owns data model, compositor, mixer, decode, export; UI owns windows/views/events.

decisions.choice: Preview and export both call jv_render_frame; playback and export both call jv_mix_audio
Core Graphics / NSImage; non-flipped view; top-down RGBA buffers
decisions.why: Deterministic upright orientation (verified with probe programs).

decisions.choice: Core Text rasterization to RGBA; in-place NSTextField editing overlay
decisions.why: Live editing with a cursor, no modal popup.

decisions.choice: AVFoundation capture + AVAudioSourceNode playback pulling the C mixer
H.264 CRF 0 (lossless) + AAC, canvas adopts first imported video resolution
AppKit NSGlassEffectView (macOS 26 SDK), no Swift/Xcode migration
decisions.why: Liquid Glass is a system material available to AppKit.

decisions.choice: Plain-text line-based .jvp + sibling <file>.assets/ for path-less media
decisions.why: Git-trackable structure; media referenced by path or sidecar PNG/WAV.

file_layout.Makefile: make / make run / make run-direct / make clean -> build/johnvideo.app
file_layout..gitignore: build output, macOS cruft, editor metadata, *.assets/, exported media
file_layout.src/engine/timeline.[ch]: data model + compositor (jv_render_frame) + mixer (jv_mix_audio) + track ops
file_layout.src/ui/AppDelegate.mm: coordinator + EditorHost + toolbar + transport + actions + save/open
file_layout.src/ui/PreviewView.mm: composited preview, canvas move/resize/rotate, in-place text edit, paste/drop
file_layout.src/ui/TimelineView.mm: ruler, lanes, clips, scrub/move/trim, track reorder/scroll, waveforms
file_layout.src/ui/Media.mm: ImageIO load + Core Text rasterize + CGImage wrap

requirements.request: Native macOS app in C + FFmpeg, Makefile, Homebrew
Copy-paste images into timeline (Cmd+V)
Drag-and-drop images from anywhere including a browser (file/bytes/URL)
Add text by right-clicking the preview or timeline
Import and composite video clips
Record voiceovers; import music; multiple audio tracks
Export the timeline to an MP4 with audio
Play/pause transport with a moving playhead
requirements.note: Wall-clock driven so visuals advance even if audio output fails.

requirements.request: Current time readout (m:ss.mmm)
Playhead snaps to clip boundaries when scrubbing
Recorded take placed at the time recording started
Fix low export quality; bilinear compositing + source-resolution canvas
Remove redundant Add Image button (Import handles it)
Recorded voiceover audible during in-app playback
requirements.note: Explicit mixer->output path + prepare; needs on-device verification.

requirements.request: Pasted/dropped images appear upright
Resize and rotate visual clips on the canvas (selection handles)
Floating bottom toolbar with Liquid Glass buttons
requirements.note: Separate rounded NSGlassEffectView capsules, not merged.

requirements.request: Fix vertical drag direction inverted
Adopt Liquid Glass without migrating to Swift/Xcode
Brighter track labels and time counter
Add and remove video/audio tracks
Move clips between tracks (drag vertically)
Horizontal timeline zoom (see more/less time)
Sticky snapping of clips to playhead and other clip boundaries on move/trim
Export at source quality (visually/mathematically lossless)
Show a waveform of recorded audio on its timeline clip
Left clip edge draggable and sticky (trim start into source)
Regular clip moves on timeline are sticky
Image moves in preview sticky to canvas edges/center
Rotations sticky to multiples of 90 degrees
Text editable with double-click on the preview
Insert text immediately; edit in place with a cursor (no popup); reflect live in timeline
Toolbar should be small/floating, not full-width; buttons round, not conjoined
Remove white line at the bottom of the window
requirements.note: Opaque dark root view + black window background; verify on-device.

requirements.request: Text rendered upright (not vertically inverted)
Save a project file
Project file format trackable in git (text)
requirements.note: Plain-text .jvp; binary media in sidecar assets dir.

requirements.request: Drag-and-drop reorder tracks vertically
All video tracks before audio tracks
Horizontal scrolling pans the timeline
Vertical scrolling moves up/down the track list
Confirm before deleting a track; warn that media disappears
Top visual track has higher z-order (drawn on top)
Add .gitignore for build artifacts and non-essential files
Flush requirements into README.md
Flush all context and user requests into readme.json
Playback still produces no sound
requirements.note: Source format now matches output (non-interleaved) and deinterleaves per-channel; verify on device.

requirements.request: Show the recorded take + waveform on the timeline in real time while recording
requirements.note: Fixed-capacity capture buffer; a live clip references it and grows each tick.

requirements.request: Bottom toolbar should float over the timeline (no reserved space), not movable
Space toggles play/stop
Left/Right arrows and h/l move the playhead in 0.5s steps
Cmd/Ctrl+C copy clip
Cmd/Ctrl+V paste (clip clipboard, else image)
Cmd/Ctrl+Z undo
Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Z redo
Ctrl + '+'/'-' zoom the timeline
't' adds text at the current position
Hierarchical project file: 2-space indented sublines, blank line after each track (neither required for parsing)

needs_on_device_verification: Recorded voiceover audible during in-app Play (export path proven; live engine output hardened but unverified headlessly)
In-place text cursor feel and whether the editing overlay looks doubled against the rendered text
Glass toolbar capsules and the bottom white-line fix render as intended
known_caveats: Lossless export (CRF 0) produces large files and slower encodes; can be dialed to a visually-lossless CRF (~12) for sane sizes on request.
Resize/rotate handle hit-areas use the unrotated position, so grabbing handles on a heavily rotated clip is approximate.
Editing clips during playback is not locked against the audio render thread (fine for single-user editing).
Video frame decoding is single-threaded (decode-on-demand at playhead); fast scrubbing of long clips may stutter.
Project save references imported media by absolute path; moving source files breaks those references (sidecar assets are relative).

build.run: make run  (launches the .app bundle so the mic permission prompt appears)
build.run_direct: make run-direct  (raw binary, for stderr logs; mic not granted this way)
